calendarNotes:
Sacramento City Unified begins the 2026-2027 school year on August 11, 2026 — earlier than most Bay Area districts, which typically start in late August. Spring Break runs March 23–27, 2027, and the school year ends May 27, 2027. Families moving to Sacramento from the Bay Area should plan for an earlier school start and an earlier summer, as the gap can be two to three weeks compared to districts like San Francisco Unified or Oakland Unified.

Sacramento metro families frequently compare SCUSD dates with neighboring districts in the region. Elk Grove Unified, Twin Rivers Unified, San Juan Unified, and Folsom Cordova Unified all serve families within driving distance of central Sacramento, and their calendars differ from SCUSD by a few days to a week in most years. Families with children in multiple Sacramento-area districts should check each district's calendar individually, especially for spring break, which tends to diverge the most.

SCUSD operates a robust open enrollment and magnet school system, including language immersion programs in Spanish, Mandarin, and other languages. Applications for these programs typically open in November or December for the following school year — well before spring break. Families interested in language immersion, arts magnets, or IB programs should begin the application process no later than early December to avoid missing enrollment windows.

Families relocating to Sacramento from the Bay Area, Southern California, or out of state often underestimate how different the Valley climate is from coastal California. Summers in Sacramento regularly reach 105°F or above, and back-to-school shopping in early August means facing peak summer heat. Plan purchases by the end of July — the week before school starts, August 4–10, is typically the hottest and most crowded for school supply shopping in the region.
